what_it_does: A static HTML showcase/catalog page that renders, in one document, every control of the design system using its `cv-*` classes: buttons (variants/sizes), cards, form fields, badges/pills/chips/tags, a comparison table, a dark-ground theme section, tabs/segmented/switch/avatar/tooltip, and a stepper/modal. It links `../styles.css` and uses design tokens (`var(--…)`) throughout for its own page chrome.
observations: Opens with a `@dsCard` HTML comment carrying gallery metadata (group, name, subtitle, viewport 1280x1640) — consumed by an external gallery/catalog tool.

Page-chrome styles in a `<style>` block use tokens (`--zone-ground`, `--ink`, `--font-display`, `--accent-bronze`, `--accent-gold`, `--r-lg`, etc.), with a couple of literal fallbacks (`var(--weight-bold,700)`, `var(--bg-dark,#1F1A12)`).
Demonstrates button variants matching the documented set: primary, ink, outline+pill, soft, ghost, comm, plus disabled.
Includes a `data-theme="dark"` wrapper labeled 'theme-invariant' showing controls on a dark ground.
Content is product-grounded real-estate/architecture copy ('Virtual Tour', 'Oakman Residences', '70+ Virtual Tours delivered', '$430B+ cost of rework', stages Design→Construction→Marketing).
Card header markup repeats the same inline `style="flex-direction:column;align-items:flex-start;gap:2px"` that Card.jsx emits.

Shows many controls not covered by the four component files in this batch (fields, pills, chips, tags, table, tabs, segmented, switch, avatar, tooltip, stepper, modal) — i.e. the system is larger than these primitives.
Accessibility attributes present in places (aria-label, role="switch", aria-checked, aria-invalid).
standouts: A single self-contained catalog of the whole control set on one page, including a deliberate dark-ground 'theme-invariant' proof section.
The `@dsCard` metadata comment integrates this page into an external gallery system.
conventions: Uses tokens (`var(--…)`) for page chrome rather than raw values, consistent with the system's token discipline.
Uses the `cv-*` BEM classes the components emit, so the showcase mirrors actual component output.
Hand-written HTML duplicates structure the components generate (e.g. the card header), a catalog/demo convention but a potential drift source.
concerns: Several hardcoded inline styles and literal fallback values (e.g. `#1F1A12`, repeated inline header flex styles, fixed px/max-width inline) sit outside the token system; given the design folder's hardcoded-literal check, these would register as hygiene flags.
Hand-authored markup mirrors component output (card header inline style), so component changes won't propagate here — demo drift risk.
notes: A one-page static gallery of the entire cv-* control library (buttons through modal), token-styled and gallery-tagged, used to view all controls and prove theme-invariance.
questions: Is this page generated/kept in sync with the components, or hand-maintained (and thus at risk of drifting from the real component output)?
What tool consumes the `@dsCard` comment and the `viewport="1280x1640"` hint?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the `@dsCard` subtitle and `<h1>`/intro state it is the component/control library showcase, and the body delivers exactly that catalog.
apparent_themes: Component catalog as a viewable artifact
intention_signals: Aims to give a single eyeball-able surface of the entire control system rendered through real tokens and classes, so a reviewer can see every control, its variants, and confirm it holds on a dark theme — turning the design system onto its own face for recognition.
novelty: Pairs a gallery-metadata comment with a hand-built but token-driven full catalog including an explicit theme-invariance section, and grounds every demo in real product copy.
contribution: Contributes the visual catalog/showcase of the full cv-* control set — the human-recognition surface for the whole component library, including the broader controls (fields, table, tabs, switch, stepper, modal) beyond the four primitive files, plus gallery integration metadata.
summary_for_embedding: A static, token-styled HTML showcase page cataloging the entire cv-* control library (buttons, cards, fields, badges, pills, chips, table, tabs, switch, stepper, modal) with a dark-theme section and gallery metadata.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a single viewable gallery of every design-system control rendered through real tokens, proving theme-invariance and serving human visual review.
